blocks.body: Present Perfect: Negatives — haven't / hasn't + V3 for actions that have not happened yet and experience that has not happened up to now.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In negative Present Perfect, we use have not / has not + V3 or the short forms haven't / hasn't.","rules":[{"label":"haven't / hasn't + V3","color":"emerald","text":"The negative is built with have / has, not with do / does.","example":"I haven't finished. / She hasn't called."},{"label":"Yet","color":"blue","text":"Yet often goes at the end of the sentence when the action is still not complete up to now.","example":"We haven't eaten yet."},{"label":"Never","color":"amber","text":"Never shows that the experience has not happened up to this moment.","example":"He has never seen snow."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic negative patterns","intro":"Start with have / has, then add not and V3.","items":[{"label":"I / You / We / They","title":"haven't + V3","subtitle":"I haven't finished. / They haven't arrived."},{"label":"He / She / It","title":"hasn't + V3","subtitle":"She hasn't called. / It hasn't started."},{"label":"Yet","title":"... yet","subtitle":"We haven't done it yet. / He hasn't replied yet."},{"label":"Full forms","title":"have not / has not","subtitle":"Both forms are correct, but haven't / hasn't usually sound more natural in speech."}]}

{"title":"2. When we need the negative","sections":[{"label":"No result yet","color":"emerald","description":"The action has still not produced a result up to now.","examples":[{"en":"I haven't found my keys yet.","ua":"The result is still missing."},{"en":"She hasn't finished her report.","ua":"The task is still not complete."}]},{"label":"No experience","color":"blue","description":"Say that someone has never had this experience.","examples":[{"en":"We have never tried this dish.","ua":"No experience up to now."},{"en":"He has never flown alone.","ua":"That experience has not happened."}]},{"label":"Unfinished period","color":"amber","description":"The state or action has not happened in a period that continues until now.","examples":[{"en":"I haven't seen her this week.","ua":"This week is not finished yet."},{"en":"They haven't lived here for long.","ua":"The period up to now is still short."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Quick patterns","intro":"See how negatives work in Present Perfect.","rows":[{"en":"I haven't finished the task yet.","ua":"haven't + finished","note":"the action is still not complete"},{"en":"She hasn't called us today.","ua":"hasn't + called","note":"today is still unfinished"},{"en":"We have never been to Berlin.","ua":"have never been","note":"experience up to now"},{"en":"He hasn't found a job yet.","ua":"hasn't + found","note":"the result is still missing"}],"warning":"Do not say I don't finished or She hasn't went."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using do / does instead of have / has.","wrong":"❌ I don't finished my work yet.","right":"✅ I haven't finished my work yet."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the wrong V3 form.","wrong":"❌ She hasn't went home.","right":"✅ She hasn't gone home."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Putting yet in the wrong place.","wrong":"❌ We haven't finished yet the task.","right":"✅ We haven't finished the task yet."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["The negative in Present Perfect = haven't / hasn't + V3.","Use haven't with I / you / we / they.","Use hasn't with he / she / it.","Yet often goes at the end of the sentence, and never shows no experience.","After haven't / hasn't, use V3."]}
